We’re in a race to see which strategy gets you to true human-like motion in AI video: pure text-to-video or motion control?  

Right now, the latter is winning. 

Using a human reference video gets you movements micro-expressions that full AI can’t yet replicate

It's a bad idea to bet against text or image-to-video models improving at scale - and we're starting to see more human-like motion from models like Seedance 2. 

IMO, the bigger problem will be teaching users how to prompt these models.
